name: Blackberry Cream Nut Tart
recipeIngredient: 16 ounces Frozen blackberries, thawed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425 degrees.
2. Combine flour and salt in a medium bowl.
3. Cut in shortening using a pastry blender or two knives until all flour is blended into pea-sized chunks.
4. Sprinkle with water, one tablespoon at a time. Toss lightly with a fork until dough forms a ball.
5. Press between hands to form a 5- to 6-inch "pancake."
6. Flour rolling surface and rolling pin lightly.
7. Roll dough into a circle. Trim 1 inch larger than an upside-down 9-inch tart pan with removable bottom.
8. Loosen dough carefully, fold into quarters, flour tart pan lightly, unfold dough, and press into tart pan.
9. Trim edge even with top of rim. Prick bottom and sides thoroughly with a fork 50 times to prevent shrinkage.
10. Cover edge with a double layer of foil to prevent over-browning.
11. Bake for 10 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool to room temperature.
12. To make cream cheese filling, combine cream cheese, condensed milk, powdered sugar, and lemon juice in a small bowl. Beat at low speed of an electric mixer until creamy.
13. Place white chocolate chips and nuts in the food processor work bowl. Process until finely chopped. Blend into cheese mixture.
14. Spread in bottom of cooled baked tart shell.
15. To make fruit filling, combine blackberries, sugar, cornstarch, and boysenberry syrup in a medium saucepan. Cook and stir on medium heat until the mixture is thickened and clear. Remove from heat. Stir in butter, lemon juice, and salt.
16. Transfer to bowl. Cool to room temperature. Spoon over cheese filling.
17. To make topping, sprinkle nuts over fruit filling in a lattice fashion.
18. To garnish, combine powdered sugar, shortening, vanilla, butter flavoring, and 3 tablespoons whipping cream in a medium bowl. Beat until smooth, adding more cream if needed for desired consistency.
19. Spoon into decorator bag fitted with the desired tip. Form decorative border around edge of tart.
20. Refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours. Remove rim. Cut into servings. Refrigerate leftovers.
Notes:
Use a 9-inch pie plate if a tart pan is unavailable.
If the tart is refrigerated longer than 2 hours, let stand at room temperature for a few minutes before serving.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
